message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary Box</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What is already known about this subject?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The Wuhan city in China had a much higher mortality rate (Feb 10
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    statistics: 748 death/18,454 diagnosis =4.05%; Apr 24
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    statistics: 3,869 death/50,333 diagnosis=7.69%) than the rest of China.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What are the new findings?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Based on our analysis, the number of infected people in Wuhan is estimated to be 143,000 (88,000 to 242,000) in late January and early February, significantly higher than the published number of diagnosed cases.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What are the recommendations for policy and practice?</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Increased awareness of the original infection rates in Wuhan, China is critically important for proper public health measures at all levels, as well as to eliminate panic caused by overestimated mortality rate that may bias health policy actions by the authorities</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: The Infection Rate of the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) in Wuhan, China

reference.unstructured: Wu Z , McGoogan JM . Characteristics of and Important Lessons From the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) Outbreak in China: Summary of a Report of 72-314 Cases From the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention. JAMA. 2020.
Oke J , Heneghan C . Global Covid-19 Case Fatality Rates UPDATED 7th April 2020. Available from: https://www.cebm.net/covid-19/global-covid-19-case-fatality-rates/.

reference.article-title: Nowcasting and forecasting the potential domestic and international spread of the 2019-nCoV outbreak originating in Wuhan, China: a modelling study
